observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to continue coordinating with the Provincial Accounting Office to verify the discrepancies of the Due from and Due to LGUs reflected in the books of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, strictly enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be valid.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office of Municipality of Teresa and Rizal Provincial Government are continuously coordinating with each other to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts of Due To/From LGU’s. As a result of reconciliation of Due To/From LGU’s-Province, the Municipality made necessary adjustments for reconciling items that require adjustments and/or remit the corresponding share of the province.
Reconciling items identified as of December 31, 2021 will be taken up and adjusted in the books in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to: a. henceforth, conduct a thorough validation on the eligibility of beneficiaries submitted by the Barangays to minimize the incurrence of problems during the implementation of any amelioration/subsidy programs of the government; and b. require the barangays to update regularly the list of beneficiaries to provide the Municipality with an accurate data for the smooth implementation of any amelioration/subsidy programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation no. 7, page 47.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant draw
the necessary journal entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts by
debiting Due to LGUs account and crediting Trust Liabilities account by
P1,800,000.00.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exercise caution in
classifying and recording transactions in order to avoid such deficiency, pursuant
to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all
the PPE including the Road Network System to visually check the existence and
condition of the properties of the Municipality; and

b. instruct the General Services Office Head to request from Engineering Office the
detailed cost of Local Road Networks and other related information to be
included in the RPCRNS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records to determine the details of the difference of
P5,214,880.57 as well as to avoid accumulation thereof, and subsequently
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to present the correct balance of
the Cash in Bank - Local Currency Account in the financial statements; and

b. Municipal Accountant to communicate with the bank and obtain bank
statements from year 1997 that could provide additional information on the
investigation of such unaccounted reconciling items reported in the BRS and
ultimately determine the cause of the unreconciled balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. issue demand letters to all accountable officers to immediately liquidate their
respective cash advances as soon as the purpose for which the advances were
granted has been served and in accordance with the prescribed period; and

b. henceforth, strictly observe the guidelines on the granting and liquidation of
cash advance pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances
of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs so that appropriate adjustments
can be made for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Municipality’s
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Accountant and the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure its accuracy so that the necessary adjustments, if there are any, could be effected at once to avoid accumulation of records; and; b. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted reconciling items amounting to P260,653.37 reported in the BRS.
observations.managementAction:
A periodic reconciliation was being made regularly by the Management. The difference as of Dec 31, 2020 was due to issued checks not yet released to Payees.
The accounting office exerted extra effort in the past, but records were not available.
observations.reason:
The difference as of Dec 31, 2020 was due to issued checks not yet released to Payees.
The accounting office exerted extra effort in the past, but records were not available. has yet to be identified as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the PPE and other affected accounts in the financial statements; and
b. exercise caution in computing and eventual recording of depreciation to ensure that the correct amount of depreciation be provided for all depreciable property of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Management already prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries covered by JEV No. 2021-05-1216 (GF) and 2021-05-0241 (SEF) both dated May 21, 2021.
The Municipal Accountant already reminded her staff to be extra careful in computing the depreciation of the Municipality’s properties moving forward.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the PPE including the Road Network System employing an alternative way to visually check the existence and condition of the property of the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period; and
b. Supply Officer to prepare the Report on Local Road Network in compliance with the regulation; coordinate with the Engineering Office for the scheduled visitation and inspection of the listed road networks and its components in order to come up with a more accurate and complete inspection report; and
c. Supply Officer to maintain complete PPE Cards for every item to account for the receipt and disposition of the same and to ensure that the custodian function is being adhered to for check and balance with the recording function of the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Supply/Property Office committed to exert more efforts in order to complete its records of PPEs in accordance with the MNGAS and submit a more reliable RPCPPE and RPCRNS moving forward.
The Municipal Supply/Property Office has coordinated with the Road Networks. The Municipal Engineer has already listed Road Networks. The Municipal Supply/Property Office has just started the issuance of updated Property Acknowledgement Report for each office that has been reconciled. After which, each property is being encoded in a Property Card using the NGAs form.
observations.reason:
The submitted Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment was incomplete and not in prescribed format.
The submitted Report on Local Road Networks was not in the prescribed format and no inspection was made in the Road Networks.
The PPE Cards has yet to be prepared as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to immediately liquidate/settle their accountabilities, if warranted, impose the withholding of salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations.
observations.managementAction: Most of the unliquidated cash advances in CY 2020 was liquidated in CY 2021 although new grants in CY 2021 remains outstanding as of December 31, 2021.
observations.reason: Most of the unliquidated cash advances in CY 2020 was liquidated in CY 2021. However, cash advances granted in CY 2021 were still unliquidated as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. BAC, thru its Secretariat, to ensure compliance with the posting requirements of all procurements in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020; b. BAC of the Municipality and all those involved in the procurement process to submit all required supporting documents, otherwise, the transaction will be suspended in audit; and c. BAC and all those involved in the procurement process to strictly comply with the procedures and documentary requirements for government procurement prescribed in the above laws and regulations; and d. Accounting Office to strictly follow the guidelines and ensure the completeness of supporting documents for each procurement transaction as required and approve and pay only those claims with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
Posting requirements were not consistently complied with since there had been series of downtime in the PhilGEPS portal, hence, the Management find it hard to log-in.
The required supporting documents were already submitted by the Management.
The required supporting documents were already submitted by the Management.
No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: The Management find it hard to log-in to PhilGEPS portal due to downtime in the portal. Some procured assets were paid despite incomplete process, documentation.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Secretariat and the BAC Chairperson to strictly comply with the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS website of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, infrastructure projects and services as required under Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to improve transparency in the government.
observations.managementAction:
The Management directed the BAC to strictly comply with the posting requirement in the PhilGEPS website of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of goods, infrastructure projects and services as required under Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to improve transparency in the government.
The required supporting documents were already submitted by the Management.
observations.reason: The required supporting documents were submitted by the Management. Posting requirements were not consistently complied by the Management in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point Person to efficiently implement the GAD PPAs in accordance with DILG endorsed GPB and regularly review and monitor the status of its implementation.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System ensured continuous coordination with offices with specific GAD PPAs for full implementation of the GPB.
observations.reason: Various GAD PPAs was implemented by the Municipality. However, full implementation was still not efficiently done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP including the list of projects and activities to be funded out of the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF to ensure the Municipality’s capacity to respond and recover from the impacts of disaster and emergencies that may occur; and
b. Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO, to prepare a comprehensive and detailed Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the same to the Audit Team within the prescribed period in compliance with the regulation.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the Management.
A comprehensive and detailed Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was already submitted by the Management.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO explained that it has been their practice to prepare and plan the investment plan of the Municipality for the current year only and did not include the previous year balances. They just issue a supplemental budget for the previous year balances as the need arise.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all of the cited reports and their supporting financial reports and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by regulations to facilitate the review and verification of the same. If not, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against him, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 (The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines).
observations.managementAction: Regular submission of the cited reports of the Municipality and supporting documents has been brought back to normal after the lockdowns due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Only minimal delays were observed on the first quarter of CY 2021 which is due to the lockdowns brought about by COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee Chairman, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to implement all programed PPAs under 20% Development Fund to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Various projects were implemented in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive obtain the approval of COA for the sale and submit the required documents to facilitate the review and determination of the validity/propriety of the sale. We also recommended to look into the cause/s of the failure of the buyer to fully pay the cost of the property within the stipulated time, and if warranted, impose the sanctions and prescribed penalties contained in the MOA or termination/ rescission of the contract relative to the sale.
observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by the Management. Retrieved the contract with the buyers and determine those who were delinquent in the payments.
Termination/Rescission still being reviewed before submission to SB for approval.
observations.reason: Rescission/Termination still being reviewed before submission to SB for approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records to ensure its accuracy so that the necessary
adjustments, if there are any, could be effected at once to avoid accumulation of
discrepancies between the two records; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted reconciling
items amounting to P260,653.37 reported in the BRS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the
PPE and other affected accounts in the financial statements; and
b. exercise caution in computing and eventual recording of depreciation to
ensure that the correct amount of depreciation be provided for all depreciable
property of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
continue coordinating with the Provincial Accounting Office to verify the
discrepancies of P(875,176.58) and P191,773.42 between the recorded balances of
the Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their respective
reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to
present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements.
Likewise, strictly enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and
payables found to be valid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the PPE
including the Road Network System employing an alternative way to visually
check the existence and condition of the property of the Municipality and
submit the RPCPPE within the prescribed period;
b. Supply Officer to prepare the Report on Local Road Network in compliance
with the regulation; and
c. Supply Officer to maintain complete PPE Cards for every item to account for
the receipt and disposition of the same and to ensure that the custodian
function is being adhered to for check and balance with the recording function
of the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal
officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to immediately
liquidate/settle their accountabilities, if warranted, impose the withholding of
salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted physical inventory of property within the office premises. However, the submitted Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment was incomplete and was submitted late to the Audit Team. Ongoing reconciliation of PPELCs and PCs. Adjusting entries were drawn under JEV No.101-20-08-02124. Errors were still found in computing depreciation. Reiterated in audit observation no. 2, page 37.
observations.reason: Management stated that spread of covid-19 greatly hampered the eventual completion of the planned physical inventory within the office premises.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments in their respective records to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank in their records; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted reconciling items amounting to P260,653.37 reported in the BRS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant made necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the account. Ongoing investigation to identify the unaccounted reconciling item.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management on this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to continue coordinating with the Provincial Accounting Office to verify the discrepancies of P841,176.52 and P28,353,056.74 between the recorded balances of the Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their respective reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, strictly enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and payables found to be valid.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office coordinated with the Provincial Accounting Office to reconcile the Due to/from LGUs account.
observations.reason: According to Management, they are still investigating the cause of the discrepancy.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. ensure that all financial transactions of the Municipality are properly recognized in the books in accordance with the prescribed accounting and auditing rules and regulations; and
b. prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the Accounts Payable and other affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that all financial transactions of the Municipality are properly recognized. Adjusting entries were made to adjust the balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LCE direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the system provider to expedite the updating of records in the newly installed system in order to facilitate the preparation and submission of the required certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible from each at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the RPT/SET Receivable in the Municipality’s books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Certified List of Taxpayers for CY 2020 was submitted to Accounting Office and set up of RPT/SET Receivables was made on Jan. 2, 2020 under JEV No. 100-20-01-002 and 200-20-01-111.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all financial reports and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by regulations to facilitate the review and verification of the same. If not, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against him, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 (The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines).
observations.managementAction: The Management strive to submit the financial reports of the cited and their supporting documents within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Compliance with the requirement for submission of the cited accounts within the prescribed period was greatly hampered by the implementation of various quarantine measures and alternative work arrangements due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Award Committee Chairman, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to implement all programmed PPAs under 20% Development Fund to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Various projects were implemented in CY 2020. However, one project is for reprogramming to other projects while another project is for implementation in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Although many projects were implemented during the year, one project is for reprogramming to other projects while another project is for implementation in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the GAD Focal Point Person to efficiently implement the GAD PPAs in accordance with DILG endorsed GPB and regularly review and monitor the status and accomplishments and the utilization of the GPB during the year of its implementation. We also recommended that the Municipality, through the LCE, should inform the DILG Provincial Office in writing the changes in the DILG endorsed GPB as required in the provisions of the said JMC.
observations.managementAction: Various GAD PPAs were implemented by the Municipality. Full implementation of GAD PPAs were still not efficiently done. Changes in the DILG endorsed GPB were communicated to DILG as required.
observations.reason: Because of the implementation of the Enhanced Community Quarantine in the Municipality, majority of the GAD PPAs that constitutes mass gathering and involves going around the community were strictly restricted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the persons liable for the immediate settlement of the amount disallowed in audit totaling P47,700.00.
observations.managementAction: Persons liable fully settled the disallowed amount in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable off to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The written request to write-off was not filed yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursement vouchers, payrolls/liquidation reports, collections and their supporting documents are completely and regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed by the mentioned regulations. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his/her salaries until he/she shall have complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against his/her, pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445 (The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines).
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Municipality still did not submit the report of documents within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to: a. properly plan and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under 20% Development Fund; and b. ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund have gone through a thorough study and priority ranking.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal officials concerned to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents to ascertain the propriety and validity of the disbursements made. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The said documents were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to continue its efforts in completing the physical count of
the properties of the Municipality and prepare and submit the required report
thereon, in proper form, summarized according to type of PPE and properly
accomplished in all particulars, in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I;
b. GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete and updated PCs
and PPELCs, respectively, and issue ARE to pinpoint accountability based on
the reconciled inventory report of PPE;
c. GSO to continue its efforts in making a complete record of all Local Road
Network of the Municipality;
d. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct the over
provision for depreciation in the Machinery and Equipment accounts and
thereafter, exercise caution in providing depreciation on PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments in
their respective records to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank in their
records; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted reconciling
items amounting to P260,653.37 reported in the BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to continue coordinating with the Provincial Accounting Office to verify the
discrepancies of P841,176.52 and P28,353,056.74 between the recorded balances
of the Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their respective
reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to
present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements.
Likewise, strictly enforce the collection and settlement for the receivables and
payables found to be valid.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. ensure that all financial transactions of the Municipality are properly
recognized in the books in accordance with the prescribed accounting and
auditing rules and regulations; and
b. prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the
Accounts Payable and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the system provider to
expedite the updating of records in the newly installed system in order to facilitate
the preparation and submission of the required certified list of taxpayers and the
tax collectible from each at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant
as a basis of recording the RPT/SET Receivable in the Municipality’s books of
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant
to submit all of the cited financial reports and their supporting documents to the
Audit Team within the period prescribed by regulations to facilitate the review and
verification of the same. If not, any unjustified failure on the part of the official
concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground for the
automatic suspension of payment of his salaries until he shall have complied
therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted
against him, pursuant to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 (The Government Auditing
Code of the Philippines).

observations.managementAction:
The Municipality started physical count of PPEs last November 2019. Physical count of PPEs was submitted to the Audit Team.
No action was taken by Management.
A Report of Local Road Network as of June 30, 2019, was submitted.
No action was taken by Management.
A disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements of Road Network System was included in 2019 Financial Reports.
observations.reason: Amount spent on construction of roads on CY 2019 were only disclosed in Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and OIC- Municipal Treasurer conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Also, necessary adjustment, if there is any, could be effected at once to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted reconciling items amounting to P260,653.37 reported in the BRS.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the required certified list of taxpayers and the amount of tax due and collectible from each at the beginning of the year as basis for the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts; and require the latter to maintain a subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer to support the recorded balances of said Receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Encoding of the list of real property owner/Taxpayers with the amount due and collectible is on-going. There are already 6 barangays completed. Three more barangays are set to be completed by year-end 2019.
observations.reason: Still no set up was made in CY 2019.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality returned the money amounting to P273,094.56 to the Bureau of Treasury and adjusted the amount of P16,370.56. Validated existing records and appropriate adjustments were made.
observations.reason: Only P16,370.56 of the Other Payables accounts remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the differences of P(944,312.38) and P5,504,975.25 between the recorded balance of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the Province’s books, and subsequently prepare the necessary journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Responsible Municipal Officials started reconciliation of records with the Provincial Accountant.
observations.reason: The unreconciled amount still existed although there was a reduction in amount compared in the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the payment from responsible municipal officials of penalties and interest incurred by the Municipality in the amount of P103,652.50 and P1,782.16 for late remittance of the amount due to the BIR and PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: Responsible Municipal Officials fully paid the penalties before December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute the SWMB to conform with the requirements provided under R.A. No. 9003. We also recommended that Municipal Mayor and the MSWMB to convene regularly to ensure that review of the SWMP will be conducted on time or as the need arises to address immediately the pressing need of the Municipality on SWM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reconstituted the composition of the SWMB to conform with the requirements provided in R.A. No. 9003. Regular meeting was conducted to properly implement the SWMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. BAC to send out written invitations to COA and at least two other observers at least three calendar days before the date of the procurement stage/activity to sit in all stages of the procurement process; b. BAC to invite qualified observers that meet the criteria set forth in the said IRR of RA No. 9184 to enhance the transparency of the bidding process; and c. BAC Secretariat to observe full compliant in posting the ITB in the PhilGEPS website to attain the widest circulation of the Municipality’s procurement/bidding activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality thru the BAC sent out written invitations to COA and other observers before the date of bidding. BAC Secretariat observed posting the ITB in PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and GAD Focal Point System to establish the GAD Database of the Municipality in accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to fully analyze the gender gaps/differences existing in the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. GAD Focal Point Person to monitor that all PPAs included in the GPB are in accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and see to it that all PPAs are implemented and funds were optimally utilized; b. GAD Focal Point Person to submit justification on the Hataw Exercise and Team Building related expenses and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the Annual GPB and GAD AR within five working days from receipt of approved plan from DILG and within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year, respectively, for timely evaluation, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made written justification on the Hataw Exercise and Team Building related expenses explaining the eligibility of such activity as GAD. The GAD Focal Point Person furnished the Audit Team of DILG endorsed GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: GAD Database not established yet. GAD Budget still not optimally utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. LDRRMC to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually and furnish the Audit Team a copy thereof, as well as the details of the transactions charged to the LDRRMF; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF and copy furnish the OCD and DILG-MLGOO in accordance with the period prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality prepared CY 2019 LDRRMFIP and copy was sent to the Audit Team. The Municipality was able to prepare and submit CY 2019 Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the required certified list of taxpayers and the amount of tax due and collectible from each at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables. Also, require the latter to maintain a subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer to support the recorded balances of said Receivables accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management, thru the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, stated that they were still in the process of encoding the data in the PATAS and will not be able to submit the required certified list of taxpayers with the amount of tax due and collectible that will be generated from the said system. She added, that they encountered problems with the encoding and already asked the assistance of their Information and Technology (IT) Department and the system provider.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer stated that they were still in the process of encoding the data in the PATAS and will not be able to submit the required certified list of taxpayers with the amount of tax due and collectible that will be generated from the said system.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the PPE
including the Road Network System of the Municipality and prepare and
submit the required report thereon, in proper form, summarized according to
type of PPE and properly accomplished in all particulars, in accordance with
Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Item VI of COA Circular No.
2015-008. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the
accounting and property records should be investigated and make the
necessary adjustments where the error was committed;
b. GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete and updated
PCs and PPELCs, respectively, and issue ARE to pinpoint accountability
based on the reconciled inventory report of PPE;
c. Municipal Engineer to expedite the submission of the complete description
and cost segregation of road components for road projects of the
Municipality;
d. GSO to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete
LRNPC for all roads and its components; and
e. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the
total road network system of the Municipality at year-end.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct
the:
a. Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Also,
necessary adjustment, if there is any, could be effected at once to avoid
accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present
the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts in the financial
statements; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to identify the unaccounted
reconciling items amounting to P260,653.37 reported in the BRS.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the required certified
list of taxpayers and the amount of tax due and collectible from each at the
beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the
books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables. Also, require the latter to maintain
a subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer to support the recorded balances of said
Receivables accounts.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the:
a. OIC-Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to
return to source agencies or to Bureau of the Treasury amount due to
National Agencies to relieve the Municipality for any accountability/liability;
b. Municipal Accountant to revert to Government Equity, with concurrence of
the Sangguniang Bayan the recorded obligation without valid documents and
have been outstanding for more than two years and record only obligation
with proper and complete documentations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to
write-off dormant receivable accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL)
and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the differences of
P(944,312.38) and P5,504,975.25 between the recorded balance of Due from
LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal
accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the Province’s books, and
subsequently prepare the necessary journal entries to present the true balance
of the said accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the payment from the
responsible municipal officials of penalties and interest incurred by the
Municipality in the amount of P103,652.50 and P1,782.16 for late remittance of
the amounts due to the BIR and PhilHealth.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to reconstitute the SWMB to
conform with the membership requirements provided under RA No. 9003.

We also recommended that Municipal Mayor and the MSWMB to convene
regularly to ensure that review of the SWMP will be conducted on time or as the
need arises to address immediately the pressing need of the Municipality on
SWM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that the disbursement vouchers, payrolls/liquidations reports, report of
collections and their supporting documents are completely and regularly
submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the period prescribed by the
mentioned regulations. Otherwise, any unjustified failure on the part of the
official concerned to submit the required reports shall be considered a ground
for the automatic suspension of payment of his/her salaries until he/she shall
have complied therewith without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may
be instituted against his/her, pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD No. 1445 (The
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) to:
a. properly plan and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the
projects under 20% Development Fund; and
b. ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund
have gone through a thorough study and priority ranking.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. BAC to send out written invitations to COA and at least two other observers
at least three calendar days before the date of the procurement stage/activity
to sit in all stages of the procurement process;
b. BAC to invite a qualified observers that meet the criteria set forth in the said
IRR of RA No. 9184 to enhance the transparency of the bidding process; and
c. BAC Secretariat to observe full compliant in posting the ITB in the PhilGEPS
website to attain the widest circulation of the Municipality’s
procurement/bidding activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal officials
concerned to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting
documents to ascertain the propriety and validity of the disbursements made.
Otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal formed an Inventory Team to undertake the physical count. Assigned personnel to facilitate the maintenance of Property Cards in the General Service Office. Assigned personnel to facilitate the maintenance of Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Office. The Municipal Mayor instructed the Supply/Property Officer to coordinate with the donors of motor vehicle for the recovering of the documents regarding the donation is on-going.
observations.reason: The audit recommendation was partially implemented as an Inventory Team was undertake the physical count. The GSO did not maintain property card for all the PPE of Municipality. The ARE on file were not yet updated. The PPELCs maintained by the Accounting is incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report starting the 2nd Semester of 2018 in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal’s BAC, through its Secretariat, will submit the report starting the 2nd Semester if 2018 and will be submitting electronically on the following month after the semester.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: a. Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the above subject accounts and remit whatever balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to employees; b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to effect timely and accurate remittance of taxes withheld, premiums contributions and loans amortization to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations; c. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments pertaining to those remittances erroneously recorded in the subsidiary ledger as compared to the actual remittance made; and d. Municipal Accountant to exert all efforts to continuously determine the composition of the unaccounted balances amounting P74,183.50 in coordination with the concerned government agencies so that appropriate disposition on said balances can be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and remit in full amounts the due to other government agencies. The Municipal Accountant analyzed the unaccounted balances and will request the reversion of the recorded obligation without valid documents and have been outstanding for more than two years.
observations.reason: Related to observation no. 7, page no. 48. The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and remit in full amounts the due to other government agencies. The Accounting office is continuously reconciling the unaccounted balances which is mostly carry over balances from prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly allocate at least one percent of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of programs of the Local Council for the Protection of Children, pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and ensure that all its program/projects/activities are implemented for the benefit of its intended recipients.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Budget Officer to comply with the requirement that at least 1% of the Municipal IRA be allocated to the strengthening of the Local Council for the Protection of Children in CY 2018 and the succeeding years.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: a. stop from approving payments of honoraria to personnel engaged in the ALS Program and other expenditures that are not among the eligible expenses chargeable to SEF, pursuant to the provisions of Dep-Ed-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.2017; b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund to transfer and replace the amount of P295,091.68 charged to the Special Education Fund; and c. direct the Municipal Accountant and the concerned official/employee to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents as stated in Annex A to substantiate the disbursements made. Thereafter, ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adhered to the provision of Dep-Ed-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s.2017. Prepared Supplemental Budget No. 2 in the GF and replace the amount of P295,091.68 charged in the SEF. The Municipal Accountant and concerned employees were directed to submit documents necessary to support the other transactions.
observations.reason:
Implemented
The budget was overlooked and not obligated for transfer to SEF.
Documents were not submitted; other transactions still pending.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated the Mayor direct: a) The Inventory Committee to complete the inventory of all supplies and fixed assets of the Municipality, prepare and submit reliable Inventory Report, in proper form and filled-up in all particulars, reconcile the amounts with the accounting records and any discrepancy should be investigated and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections where the error is committed, and record all items found during the inventory which are not recorded in the books of accounts. b) The Accountant and acting Property Officer to maintain a complete and accurate stock ledger cards and stock cards, property cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment c)  the officials concerned to get from the government agencies/donors concerned the documents evidencing the transfer of ownership of the 3 motor vehicles as a basis of recording their costs in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Install an Application System for Supply Management.
observations.reason:
Computerization program not yet completed.
Reiterated under Observation No.2

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Accountant the duly certified list of taxpayers and the tax due from each every beginning of each year, so that the same could be recorded in the books of accounts. We also recommended that Management find ways in solving the identified causes of the perennial failure of the Treasurer to prepare and submit the aforecited list, which is the basis of the Accountant in recording the RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had enter into contract with a System Provider to handle the computerization system of its operation that started on the fourth quarter of 2016. Through this, the office may generate the listing as required to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis for setting up the RPT and SET Receivables for the year 2018.
observations.reason: Computerization program not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

 1. Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile at the end of each month
    their bank account records with that of the bank balance by
    immediately taking up in the books all reconciling items that need
    to be recorded; and


 2. Accountant to dig deeper on the records or exert extra efforts to
    identify the “unaccounted reconciling items” on the BRS in the
    amount of             P260,653.37 and record the stale checks to
    correct the Cash in Bank balance as well as the accounts payable
    and henceforth revert immediately back to cash in bank all stale
    checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

 1.a   Inventory Committee to complete the inventory  of  all  supplies
     and fixed assets of  the  Municipality;  and  submit  the  properly
     accomplished Inventory Reports as require under Section 57  of  the
     MNGAS for LGUs  Volume  II  and  reconcile  the  Reports  with  the
     accounting and property records, investigate any  discrepancy  that
     may exist thereafter and effect  the  necessary  corrections  where
     needed;


 1.b prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable  Property
     for  those  unserviceable  properties  and  follow  the  rules  and
     regulation on the disposal of unserviceable properties;


 2.a   the General Service Office to immediately record the  deliveries
     of supplies and property based on  the  Acceptance  and  Inspection
     Report (AIR) and other supporting documents and forward the same to
     the Municipal Accountant for preparation  of  Disbursement  Voucher
     and recording of deliveries to appropriate ledger cards;


 2.b   the General Service Office and the Accounting Office to maintain
     updated  and  complete  stock  cards/property  cards  and  ARE  and
     supplies/property  ledger   cards,   respectively   to   facilitate
     inventory  taking,  reconciliation   between   the   property   and
     accounting records  and  verification  of  the  Inventory  and  PPE
     accounts. Determine the officials/employees to whom  the  equipment
     were issued, and issue the  corresponding  Acknowledgement  Receipt
     for Equipment (ARE) for each property to pinpoint the direct person
     accountable and responsible thereon.  Moreover, renew all ARE every
     three   years   to   update   signature   and   information    like
     designation/position or assignment  of  the  holder  of  each  item
     listed in the RPCPPE;


 3.    Officials concerned to make representation from the Province  of
     Rizal for the documents needed for recording the  donated  vehicles
     in the book of accounts of the municipality.  Exert extra effort to
     find any documents for the  Montero  vehicle  for  it  was  already
     registered in the name of the municipality and or inquire the  cost
     from the dealer/manufacturer of the vehicle  on  the  year  it  was
     given or year model and be the basis for  recording  the  cost  and
     subsequently compute the accumulated depreciation.

a. The officers/employees with outstanding cash advances  be  directed
    to immediately liquidate/settle the same.  If  warranted,  withhold
    their salaries to answer  for  their  unliquidated  cash  advances,
    pursuant to the above regulation.


 b. The Management should strictly enforce the time limits in the
    liquidation of cash advances and ensure that no additional cash
    advances are granted to officials unless their previous cash
    advances are fully liquidated or settled.

We still reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the
Municipal Treasurer furnishes the Accountant the duly certified list
of taxpayers and the tax due every beginning of each year as her basis
to recognize the related receivable accounts.

1.    Accountable officer to submit the  documentary  requirement  for
   each type of disbursements and justification on  the  inconsistency
   of the number of participants during Solidarity Meeting versus  the
   approved Purchase Request to avoid suspension  and/or  disallowance
   in audit;


2.     Municipal Accountant to verify the liquidation and  disbursement
   vouchers as to legality and  the  documentary  requirements  before
   recording in the books and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

a. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant  to  ensure  that
   only investment or capital expenditures are charged to the  20%
   Development Fund;


b.  Municipal  Accountant  observe  the   proper   classification   of
   transactions  in  accordance  with  the  Philippine  Public  Sector
   Accounting Standard.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the  Municipal
Treasurer, in coordination  with  the  accountant,  to  remit  to  the
BTr/source agencies all the unused balance of funds  pursuant  to  the
above regulation so that the amount  could  be  immediately  used  for
other projects/programs of the National Government and to relieve  the
municipality from any accountability/liability  for  the  said  unused
funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the
GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), direct the GAD Technical Working  Group
(TWG) or secretariat which is composed of various offices  within  the
Municipality to fast-track the  completion  of  the  GAD  Database  by
coming together  and  actively  participate  in  the  development  and
maintenance of the same, which would contain gender statistics and sex-
disaggregated data that should be systematically  gathered,  regularly
updated and subjected to gender analysis  for  planning,  programming,
and policy formulation in compliance with  Section  4.1.B.1  of  Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, to ensure that GAD programs, projects
and activities included in the GAD Plan.

                 We  also  recommended  that  the  establishment  or   the
identification of Gender Issue and/or GAD Mandate  reflecting  on  the
Annual GAD Plan and Budget shall be aligned to the Philippine Plan for
Gender-Responsive Development, 1995-2025.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires the:

   a. LDRRM officer to ensure that the  LDRRMF  is  used  only  for
      activities related to  disaster  risk  management  activities
      pursuant to existing regulations; and


   b. Municipal Accountant to properly record  the  items  procured
      for stocking  in  anticipation  for  future  relief  operation,
      to  its appropriate inventory accounts and  to  expense  accounts
      only upon issuance of items to end user.

                 We also recommend immediate refund of the disallowed amount
   of     P 128,155.00.

We strongly reiterated that Management require: a) all officers/employees with unliquidated cash advances, including those who are no longer employees of the Municipality, to immediately liquidate/settle the same. If Necessary, withhold the salaries of those who continually fail or refuse to comply with the repeated demands and requests for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them, to compel liquidation thereof, and b) the close monitoring of all unliquidated cash advances and demand immediately the liquidation of the same; strictly enforce the time limits in the liquidation of cash advances; ensure that no additional cash advances are granted to officials unless their previous cash advances are fully liquidated and settled, duly certified by the Accountant, and cash advances, except for travelling expenses, be granted only to duly bonded employees.
observations.managementAction: All employees with unliquidated cash advances were properly notified both verbally and in writing. The amount of P242,682.85 were fully liquidated on CY 2016. The management will enforce the time limits in the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No.3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit all the required documents for the above disbursements to substantiate the payments made. We also strongly reiterated that Management see to it that all procurement are duly supported with the proper and complete documents required by regulations before processing, approving and paying claims.
observations.managementAction: To submit the required documents for the above disbursements to substantiate the payments made. To ensure that all procurement is duly supported with proper and complete documents required.
observations.reason: Verification on-going. Not all documents were submitted. Subject to audit suspension. Samples were taken for the procurement of infrastructures. Some documents were not attached, mostly to losing bidders, however, not accounted on the checklist of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the job order/labor contracts of all the job order employees hired during the year, to substantiate the payments made. We also recommended that Management should pay only the actual number of days the employees have rendered service and require the refund of the excess amounts paid. Ensure that the daily time records are properly signed by both the employees and their immediate supervisor before paying the same.
observations.managementAction: To submit Job order/labor contracts for the period January-December, 2015 including the contract of service of Ariel Esguerra and Mr Peter Lawrence Nava. The concerned officials were instructed to ensure that the DTRs are properly signed both by the employee and the immediate supervisor.
observations.reason: Verification on-going. Not all documents were submitted. Some actions taken; concerned officials instructed. Samples were taken; some items not fully accounted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the officials concerned ensure that the SEF is used only for purposes it was created and in accordance with the above-mentioned regulations. Also the municipal and school officials concerned should be required to justify and submit the complete documents for the expenses paid out of the SEF, to avoid audit suspension or audit allowance. We also recommended that the officials concerned be required to provide the legal basis on the payment of Performance Incentive Benefits to its officials/employees, despite the prohibition in the cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The School Board Officials and other concerned employees were instructed to strictly adhere to SEF rules on the utilization of SEF. After an AOM in June, 2016 was received, items of the same nature found in Audit were no longer incurred.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No.5

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials concerned be required to provide the legal basis on the payment of Performance Incentive Benefits to its officials/employees, despite the prohibition in the cited regulations. Also the municipal and school officials concerned should be required to justify and submit the complete documents for the expenses paid out of the SEF, to avoid audit suspension or audit allowance.
observations.managementAction: To remit the Barangay and Provincial shares from RPT and SET collections within 5 days after the end of each quarter and at least once a week respectively. Project proponents to implement their identified GAD projects as scheduled. Complete filling up of GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.reason: Verification still on-going as to the validity and sufficiency of the basis.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that management remit to the Barangay and the Province their shares from the RPT collections within 5 days after the end of each quarter and at least once a week, respectively. Henceforth, comply strictly with the laws, rules and regulations on the payment of allowances and other benefits to the officials/employees of the Municipality to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: To remit the Barangay and Provincial shares from RPT and SET collections within 5 days after the end of each quarter and at least once a week respectively.
observations.reason: They contend that the remittance of RPT and SET collections on the date prescribed by RA 7160, etc. is not possible during the first quarter due to closing of the books, and increase in volume of transactions in December because taxpayers availed of 20% discount on advance payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the additional funds could be immediately used to finance their projects/programs for the benefit of their constituents. To maintain Special Account for Municipality’s loan in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete Subsidiary Ledgers and preparation of the financial statements, pursuant to the above regulations.
observations.managementAction: To maintain Special Account for Municipality’s loan in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that Management direct the Accountant to maintain a special account for the Municipality’s loans in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete Subsidiary Ledgers and preparation of the financial statements, pursuant to the above regulations. Identify and address the cause/s of the continued failure of the Accountant to maintain the required special account. To include the insurance premiums in the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: To insure with GSIS, vehicles donated by other agency.
observations.reason: Not all properties were insured by the agency especially for the donated properties to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure all properties of the Municipality with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS, so that in case of loss or destruction of these properties the Municipality could at least be compensated and recover the cost of these assets. We recommended also that the funds for the insurance premiums are included in the annual budget of the Municipality. Ensure in the Annual General Fund appropriations that 5% will be allotted for GAD. To submit Reports of Collection, Deposit and Report of check Issued and the Bank Reconciliation Statement on the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Ensure in the Annual General Fund appropriations that 5% will be allotted for GAD. To submit Reports of Collection, Deposit and Report of check Issued and the Bank Reconciliation Statement on the prescribed period.
observations.reason: They contend that the prescribed period for submission of reports during the first quarter is not attainable due to closing of Books of Accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation, that the Mayor direct the Accountant to submit of all the cited financial records and reports and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the period prescribed by regulations to facilitate the review and verification of the reports and the timely preparation of the required audit report. We also reiterated that the Municipality provides at least five percent (5%) of its annual General Fund appropriations for GAD related activities and ensure that all its planned programs/projects/ activities to address gender issues are implemented for the benefit of its constituents. The official concerned should look into possible causes of the low implementation of GAD programs and find solutions thereto to maximize the utilization of the Gad budget. We also recommended that the actual results on the GAD activities undertaken/implemented be properly indicated in the Accomplishment Report and the targets and accomplishments, as much as possible, be quantified to facilitate assessment of the Municipality’s attainment of its targets.
observations.managementAction: Project proponents to implement their identified GAD projects as scheduled. Complete filling up of GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No.8
